title: Affect-Aware ASR Stack
subtitle: Keep words, acoustic affect, context, and policy as separate channels.

nodes.label: Speech Front End
nodes.description: VAD, denoising, segmentation, diarization, and audio quality flags.
nodes.evidence: Research digest: The Affect-Aware ASR Stack

nodes.label: ASR Branch
nodes.description: Text, timestamps, confidence, word errors, and n-best alternatives.
nodes.evidence: Research digest: Why Emotion Belongs Next To ASR

nodes.label: Acoustic Affect
nodes.description: Prosody, arousal, valence, categorical probabilities, and uncertainty.
nodes.evidence: Research digest: What Each Signal Can And Cannot Tell You

nodes.label: Context Fusion
nodes.description: Combine audio, transcript, turn history, speaker context, and confidence.
nodes.evidence: Research digest: A Useful Architecture Pattern

nodes.label: Policy Layer
nodes.description: Decide whether to expose, store, aggregate, suppress, or abstain.

edges.label: words
edges.evidence: ASR produces lexical content and confidence.

edges.label: voice cues
edges.evidence: Affect also lives in prosody and voice quality.

edges.label: text evidence
edges.evidence: Transcript features help only when ASR uncertainty is preserved.

edges.label: acoustic evidence
edges.evidence: Acoustic affect should stay separate from text.
